text: A student accidentally touches a hot stove and quickly pulls the hand away before feeling pain. Which part of the nervous system is primarily responsible for this immediate response?
rationale: The spinal cord mediates reflex actions before the brain consciously processes the pain.

text: A client has difficulty breathing because of damage to the diaphragm. Which nerve is most likely affected?
rationale: The phrenic nerve stimulates the diaphragm which is the primary muscle of respiration.

text: A nurse notices a client's heart rate increases during exercise. Which physiological response explains this change?
rationale: Exercise activates the sympathetic nervous system causing an increased heart rate to meet oxygen demands.

text: A client is dehydrated after prolonged vomiting. Which organ responds by conserving water through urine concentration?
rationale: The kidneys regulate fluid balance by concentrating urine to conserve water.

text: A patient experiences difficulty maintaining balance after a head injury. Which part of the brain is most likely affected?
rationale: The cerebellum coordinates balance posture and voluntary movement.

text: A client eats a meal rich in carbohydrates. Which hormone is released to lower the blood glucose level?
rationale: Insulin promotes glucose uptake by cells and lowers blood glucose levels.

text: A patient with anemia has reduced oxygen delivery to tissues. Which blood component is decreased?
rationale: Red blood cells contain hemoglobin which transports oxygen throughout the body.

text: A nurse explains that digestion begins in the mouth. Which substance starts the breakdown of starches?
rationale: Salivary amylase begins carbohydrate digestion in the mouth.

text: A client has a fracture involving the femur. Which body system is primarily affected?
rationale: The femur is the longest bone in the skeletal system.

text: A patient is diagnosed with hypothyroidism. Which gland is functioning below normal?
rationale: The thyroid gland produces hormones that regulate metabolism.

text: A client has swelling after twisting an ankle. Which blood cells help initiate the inflammatory response?
rationale: White blood cells protect the body by participating in inflammation and immune responses.

text: A nurse explains that oxygen enters the bloodstream in the lungs. In which structure does this gas exchange occur?
rationale: The alveoli are tiny air sacs where oxygen and carbon dioxide are exchanged.

text: A patient has liver disease. Which major function of the liver may be impaired?
rationale: The liver detoxifies harmful substances and performs many metabolic functions.

text: A client with severe bleeding has low blood pressure. Which physiological mechanism helps restore blood pressure first?
rationale: The body increases heart rate through sympathetic stimulation to maintain cardiac output.

text: A patient is unable to extend the knee after an injury. Which muscle group is most likely affected?
rationale: The quadriceps muscle group is responsible for knee extension.

text: A nurse explains why calcium is important. Which body system depends on calcium for proper muscle contraction?
rationale: Calcium is essential for normal muscle contraction and nerve transmission.

text: A client has damage to the kidneys. Which waste product may accumulate in the blood?
rationale: The kidneys remove urea from the bloodstream through urine formation.

text: A patient has difficulty seeing in dim light. Which part of the eye is primarily responsible for vision in low light?
rationale: Rod cells are highly sensitive to low levels of light and provide night vision.

text: A client with constipation is encouraged to increase dietary fiber. In which organ is most water absorbed from digested food?
rationale: The large intestine absorbs water from undigested food and forms feces.

text: A patient complains of rapid fatigue during physical activity. Which organ supplies oxygenated blood to the muscles to support activity?
rationale: The heart pumps oxygen-rich blood throughout the body to meet the metabolic needs of muscles during activity.
